metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the staged opt-in config mechanism: name the format (e.g. a `rebar.voter.toml` `[projects]` allowlist, or a Gerrit `project.config` label ACL), where it lives, and the exact field an operator sets to enable the voter for a given project.
findings.finding: The staged opt-in config mechanism is underspecified: 'per-project/staged opt-in (config gate)' names the concept but does not specify the config format, location (e.g. repo-level YAML, Gerrit project.config stanza, environment variable, server-side allowlist), or the data model for which projects are opted in. An implementer would have to 'choose an appropriate' mechanism — a deferred design choice.
findings.location: Plan-review remediation section: 'Voter activation is staged (per-project/config gate) before fleet-wide' / Added AC: 'Voter activation is staged (per-project/config opt-in) before fleet-wide; documented'
findings.suggested_fix: Add one sentence to the Specifics section, e.g.: 'Opt-in is controlled by a `voter.enabled_projects` list in `rebar-mcp/config.toml`; an empty list means no projects are active (safe default). Fleet-wide activation is a separate config change.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the per-project opt-in mechanism: name the config key or receiver-level allow-list (e.g. a `voter.enabled_projects` list in the receiver config, or the existing `verify.enable_code_review` in each project's rebar.toml) so the staged rollout AC is implementable without design invention.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a 'per-project/staged opt-in (config gate)' for voter activation but does not specify the mechanism. The existing code_review_enabled() reads rebar.toml at the repo_root of the cloned change — correct for rebar's own repo, but the receiver processes Gerrit projects generically. The plan doesn't clarify whether 'per-project' is a receiver-level allow-list (e.g. a list of Gerrit project names in the receiver's own config file), or whether it relies on the checked-out project's rebar.toml. Without this, an implementer must invent the gating mechanism, which could lead to either: (a) accidentally activating on all Gerrit projects by default if rebar.toml is absent, or (b) silently doing nothing for projects without rebar.toml. Since the staged-rollout AC was added specifically to prevent an under-tested voter from gating ALL committers on day one, the mechanism matters.
findings.location: Plan-review remediation (Round-11) — 'Staged rollout' bullet / Added AC: 'Voter activation is staged (per-project/config opt-in) before fleet-wide; documented'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Split WS6 into two child tickets: WS6-a (voter integration + stub-Gerrit tests, unblocked) and WS6-b (dogfood proof, blocked on d251); each has its own ACs and can be closed independently.
findings.finding: The leaf ticket spans ≥3 layers (event wiring, HTTP REST client, adapter seam, staged rollout config gate), introduces a new interface (`code_review_decision` adapter), carries 6 ACs, and contains two deliverables with structurally different blocking conditions (stub-testable integration vs. d251-gated dogfood). The plan itself labels these 'two separable deliverables,' yet keeps them in one leaf ticket — too large to execute coherently in one session.
findings.location: Specifics pinned / 'Two separable deliverables' paragraph + full AC list
findings.suggested_fix: WS6-a ACs: event wiring fires on patchset-created, REST vote succeeds against stub Gerrit, adapter seam implemented, staged opt-in config gate passes unit tests, fail-closed label verified in stub. WS6-b ACs: at least one real rebar change reviewed + voted on AWS/Gerrit env (record URL + vote).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define and document an explicit rollback path for the voter: the mechanism to disable it quickly (e.g., flip the per-project config, or a master kill-switch), what happens to existing votes on open changes, and a tested procedure (even manual) to revert without data cleanup.
findings.finding: No explicit rollback path is specified for the voter. If the voter malfunctions (e.g., false BLOCKs fleet-wide), the plan does not describe how to disable it quickly, what the disablement mechanism is, or whether disabling it is tested.
findings.location: Plan-review remediation — 'Staged rollout' AC; Acceptance Criteria
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC: 'A per-project opt-out/disable config toggle is documented and tested; flipping it to disabled stops new votes immediately (no restart required) and the runbook notes that existing -1 votes on open changes must be manually rescored to 0 by the service account.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the per-project opt-in config's full lifecycle: where it lives, its schema, who owns updates (single owner or PR-gated file), and a retirement path for decommissioned projects.
findings.finding: The per-project opt-in config (the staged rollout gate) is shared/mutable state whose lifecycle is unspecified: who creates entries, who owns updates, what format/schema it has, and whether there is a retirement path when a project is decommissioned.
findings.location: Plan-review remediation — 'Staged rollout: the voter activates behind a per-project/staged opt-in (config gate)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the voter's non-happy-path behavior: (a) retry/timeout policy for the Gerrit REST vote call, (b) what label value and robot comment are posted when the gate itself errors or times out (e.g., -1 labeled as 'voter-infrastructure failure' consistent with the existing fail-closed-distinct convention).
findings.finding: The plan does not specify how the voter behaves when the Gerrit REST vote call itself fails (network error, auth failure, HTTP 4xx/5xx), or when the four-pass gate times out — leaving committers with no vote, no explanation, and potentially a stuck submit requirement.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria / Specifics pinned — voter integration
findings.suggested_fix: Extend the fail-closed-distinct AC to cover voter-layer failures: on REST call failure or gate timeout, post LLM-Review -1 with a robot comment tagged [voter-error] (distinct from [coverage-gap] and [real-finding]), and log the error for operator triage. Define a max retry count (e.g., 2 retries with exponential backoff) before giving up and posting the error -1.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add explicit failure-path ACs for the REST vote call: specify behavior on 403/auth-failure, 429/rate-limit, 5xx/timeout (e.g., retry with backoff, surface a distinct robot comment, or re-raise so the event is requeued), and add a corresponding test against the stub Gerrit.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new user-facing flow — Gerrit REST vote on patchset-created — but specifies no failure/error paths: what happens when the REST vote call returns 403 (auth failure), 429 (rate limit), 5xx (Gerrit unavailable), or times out. With no error-path handling specified, the voter may silently drop votes, crash the handler, or produce confusing partial states on Gerrit.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1 / Specifics pinned — Vote mapping

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add INDETERMINATE explicitly to the vote-mapping statement and to AC1: 'BLOCK or INDETERMINATE (LLM outage/degradation) → LLM-Review -1 (fail-closed); PASS → +1/MAX' — the adapter absorbs the three-valued verdict before it reaches the REST call.
findings.finding: The WS6 plan's vote mapping and ACs only mention BLOCK and PASS/advisory as cases, but produce_code_review_verdict (which WS6's adapter wraps) emits three verdicts: PASS, BLOCK, and INDETERMINATE. The adapter collapses these to {PASS|BLOCK} — a correct design — but WS6's own AC1 ('block/advisory maps to the label value') and the 'Specifics pinned' vote-mapping statement never explicitly state that INDETERMINATE must map to BLOCK (-1, fail-closed). Since WS6 owns the adapter implementation over produce_code_review_verdict, the INDETERMINATE→BLOCK rule is a load-bearing mechanism that the plan leaves implicit. An implementer reading only WS6 could emit a +0 abstain or raise an exception on INDETERMINATE rather than -1.
findings.location: Specifics pinned — Vote mapping / AC: 'On patchset-created…block/advisory maps to the label value'
findings.suggested_fix: In 'Specifics pinned — Vote mapping': change to 'deterministic BLOCK → -1; INDETERMINATE (LLM outage / mid-run degradation) → -1 (fail-closed); PASS/advisory → +1/MAX'. In AC1: 'block/INDETERMINATE/advisory maps to the label value per the fail-closed rule above'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add INDETERMINATE to the WS6 vote-mapping table with an explicit rule (e.g. INDETERMINATE → decision=BLOCK → `LLM-Review -1`, fail-closed), co-located with the BLOCK and PASS rules in the 'Specifics pinned / Vote mapping' section.
findings.finding: The WS6 vote-mapping section covers only BLOCK → -1 and PASS/advisory → +1/MAX. The produce_code_review_verdict function (confirmed in code_review_verdict.schema.json) returns a third enum value INDETERMINATE — but WS6's vote-mapping table never specifies what label value an INDETERMINATE result casts. The d251 epic's mapping (PASS=+MAX, BLOCK/INDETERMINATE=-1) is in d251's body, not re-stated in WS6, leaving the adapter implementer without a WS6-local decision rule for this case.
findings.location: Plan section 'Specifics pinned' — 'Vote mapping' bullet; and AC 'block/advisory maps to the label value'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Vote mapping bullet: 'INDETERMINATE (LLM outage / mid-run degradation) → BLOCK → `LLM-Review -1` (fail-closed; same as d251's BLOCK/INDETERMINATE=-1 rule).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the 'PASS/advisory → +1/MAX' ambiguity: state the exact Gerrit integer for each decision outcome (PASS→+MAX, advisory findings present but no BLOCK→+MAX or +1?), confirm it satisfies `label:LLM-Review=MAX`, and anchor 'advisory' to its source (code_review_verdict.advisory array, not a decision enum value).
findings.finding: The WS6 vote-mapping table writes 'PASS/advisory → `LLM-Review +1/MAX`' — a slash-delimited notation that is ambiguous: it is unclear whether PASS maps to +MAX and advisory maps to +1 (two distinct integer values), or both map to the same value. The submit requirement from d251 is `label:LLM-Review=MAX` — a vote of +1 that is not MAX would NOT satisfy the submit requirement, making the distinction load-bearing. No single canonical integer mapping for the advisory outcome is stated anywhere in the WS6 plan.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'PASS/advisory → `LLM-Review +1/MAX`' with 'decision=PASS (regardless of advisory findings) → `LLM-Review` +MAX (the label's maximum value, satisfying the `=MAX` submit requirement); advisory findings are surfaced in the robot comment only, not in the vote integer.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC covering deliverable (a): 'voter integration (event wiring + REST vote) passes an automated test suite against a stub/mock Gerrit — verifiable before d251 lands.'
findings.finding: The plan body explicitly identifies 'voter integration testable against a STUB Gerrit now' as a separable, earlier deliverable, but no acceptance criterion requires stub-based tests to pass (or to exist) — leaving this intermediate validation state uncovered by any verifiable criterion.
findings.location: Specifics pinned — 'Two separable deliverables' paragraph; Acceptance Criteria section
findings.suggested_fix: Add AC: '[ ] Voter integration is covered by automated tests against a stub Gerrit (mock REST endpoint); these tests pass in CI independently of the d251 environment.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the robot comment format/vocabulary that distinguishes a coverage-gap/infra-failure -1 from a real-finding -1 (e.g. a required prefix, tag, or structured field such as `[INFRA-FAILURE]` vs `[REVIEW-FINDING]`), co-located with the fail-closed AC.
findings.finding: The plan's AC states 'A fail-closed/coverage-gap -1 is surfaced distinctly from a real-finding -1 in the Gerrit vote/robot comment,' but no schema, template, or vocabulary is provided for what the robot comment must contain to achieve this distinction. The adapter return type {decision, message, findings} (from d251 Round-11) provides a 'message' field, but WS6 does not define what the message string must say to be recognizably a coverage-gap/infra-failure comment vs. a real-finding comment — leaving the 'distinct' requirement unspecified at the structural level an implementer needs.
findings.location: Plan-review remediation section — AC: 'A fail-closed/coverage-gap -1 is surfaced distinctly from a real-finding -1 in the Gerrit vote/robot comment'

findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an ADR or CLAUDE.md section capturing the Gerrit voter architecture: event wiring, the `code_review_decision` adapter seam contract, vote-mapping rules, and fail-closed distinction logic.
findings.finding: No ADR or design-doc update is planned to capture the novel Gerrit voter integration architecture (event wiring, REST vote contract, adapter seam, label-vote mapping, fail-closed distinction). This is a cross-component pipeline stage introducing a new contract.
findings.location: Specifics pinned / Plan-review remediation sections (no documentation artifact mentioned)
findings.suggested_fix: Create `docs/adr/0006-gerrit-llm-review-voter.md` covering: (1) the adapter seam signature and why it's the single swap point, (2) the BLOCK/PASS→label-value mapping and its immutability contract, (3) the per-project opt-in config schema, and (4) the fail-closed labeling convention for coverage-gap -1s.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
